title: Poison failed EDHOC sessions
description: EDHOC processing errors after DH retain PRKs and retryable state; create_message_1 can reuse invalid/zeroized ephemeral state; injected RNG constructors use infallible fill_bytes and may panic on hardware entropy failure. Make failures immediately zeroize and permanently poison the session, guard all lifecycle methods, and use fallible entropy APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Every post-DH parser/authentication error zeroizes stored/transient secrets and prevents retry/export; create_message_1 rejects completed/failed/zeroized state; try_fill_bytes entropy failure returns an explicit error without panic or partial object; host/Cortex-M tests and three reviews pass.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed lifecycle poisoning, fallible injected entropy, immediate raw/stored secret wiping, and state guards; host and Cortex-M checks plus three exact reviews pass.
